what: Bootstrapped cluster-based permutation analysis for time series data with GLM.jl and MixedModels.jl
title: Bootstrapped cluster-based permutation analysis for time series data with GLM.jl and MixedModels.jl
text: Julia GLM.jl MixedModels.jl implementation bootstrapped cluster-based permutation analysis time series data, powered JuliaConnectoR

title: Bootstrapped cluster-based permutation analysis for time series data with GLM.jl and MixedModels.jl
text: can install development version jlmerclusterperm GitHub : Using jlmerclusterperm requires prior installation Julia, can downloaded either official website via command line utility juliaup. Julia version >=1.8 required >=1.9 preferred substantial compiler/runtime improvements. using functions jlmerclusterperm, initial setup step required via calling jlmerclusterperm_setup(). launch Julia number threads (controlled via options("jlmerclusterperm.nthreads")) install necessary Julia package dependencies (happens take 15-30 minutes). Subsequent calls jlmerclusterperm_setup() incurs overhead around 30 seconds experience small delays first-time function calls due Julia’s just--time compilation. Afterwards can enjoy blazingly-fast functions package.
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("yjunechoe/jlmerclusterperm") # Both lines must be run library(jlmerclusterperm) system.time(jlmerclusterperm_setup(verbose = FALSE)) #>    user  system elapsed  #>    0.00    0.03   19.98

title: Bootstrapped cluster-based permutation analysis for time series data with GLM.jl and MixedModels.jl

title: Bootstrapped cluster-based permutation analysis for time series data with GLM.jl and MixedModels.jl
text: jlmerclusterperm functions collect Julia objects R objects, except jlmer to_jlmer return GLM.jl MixedModels.jl fitted model objects. Model output comparable lme4 models: can use functions JuliaConnectoR interact (pointers ) Julia objects: can also call functions Julia packages already installed. example, Effects.jl marginaleffects/emmeans-like features: following packages loaded Julia environment via jlmerclusterperm_setup(): wish use Julia packages listed like Effects.jl, recommend install global library fresh Julia session opposed Pkg.add()-ing R using JuliaConnectoR interface, may pollute jlmerclusterperm project environment (happens, just re-install package).
code: jmod <- to_jlmer(Reaction ~ Days + (Days | Subject), lme4::sleepstudy) jmod #> <Julia object of type LinearMixedModel{Float64}> #> Variance components: #>             Column    Variance Std.Dev.   Corr. #> Subject  (Intercept)  565.51066 23.78047 #>          Days          32.68212  5.71683 +0.08 #> Residual              654.94145 25.59182 #>  ────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>                 Coef.  Std. Error      z  Pr(>|z|) #> ────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> (Intercept)  251.405      6.63226  37.91    <1e-99 #> Days          10.4673     1.50224   6.97    <1e-11 #> ────────────────────────────────────────────────── glance(jmod) # see also `tidy()` #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>    nobs sigma logLik   AIC   BIC deviance df.residual #>   <int> <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl>       <int> #> 1   180  25.6  -876. 1764. 1783.    1752.         174 library(lme4) rmod <- lmer(Reaction ~ Days + (Days | Subject), sleepstudy) summary(rmod) #> Linear mixed model fit by REML ['lmerMod'] #> Formula: Reaction ~ Days + (Days | Subject) #>    Data: sleepstudy #>  #> REML criterion at convergence: 1743.6 #>  #> Scaled residuals:  #>     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max  #> -3.9536 -0.4634  0.0231  0.4634  5.1793  #>  #> Random effects: #>  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev. Corr #>  Subject  (Intercept) 612.10   24.741        #>           Days         35.07    5.922   0.07 #>  Residual             654.94   25.592        #> Number of obs: 180, groups:  Subject, 18 #>  #> Fixed effects: #>             Estimate Std. Error t value #> (Intercept)  251.405      6.825  36.838 #> Days          10.467      1.546   6.771 #>  #> Correlation of Fixed Effects: #>      (Intr) #> Days -0.138 library(JuliaConnectoR) juliaLet("x.rePCA", x = jmod) #> <Julia object of type NamedTuple{(:Subject,), Tuple{Vector{Float64}}}> #> (Subject = [0.5406660465696776, 1.0],) juliaCall("issingular", jmod) #> [1] FALSE juliaEval("using Effects") juliaLet("effects(x.namedelements, y)", x = list(Days = unique(sleepstudy$Days)), y = jmod) #> <Julia object of type DataFrame> #> 10×5 DataFrame #>  Row │ Days     Reaction  err       lower    upper #>      │ Float64  Float64   Float64   Float64  Float64 #> ─────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────── #>    1 │     0.0   251.405   6.63226  244.773  258.037 #>    2 │     1.0   261.872   6.59566  255.277  268.468 #>    3 │     2.0   272.34    6.89435  265.445  279.234 #>    4 │     3.0   282.807   7.48832  275.319  290.295 #>    5 │     4.0   293.274   8.31451  284.96   301.589 #>    6 │     5.0   303.742   9.31132  294.43   313.053 #>    7 │     6.0   314.209  10.4299   303.779  324.639 #>    8 │     7.0   324.676  11.6353   313.041  336.311 #>    9 │     8.0   335.143  12.9031   322.24   348.046 #>   10 │     9.0   345.611  14.2167   331.394  359.827 cat(readLines(system.file(package = "jlmerclusterperm", "julia", "Project.toml")), sep = "\n") #> [deps] #> DataFrames = "a93c6f00-e57d-5684-b7b6-d8193f3e46c0" #> GLM = "38e38edf-8417-5370-95a0-9cbb8c7f171a" #> MixedModels = "ff71e718-51f3-5ec2-a782-8ffcbfa3c316" #> ProgressMeter = "92933f4c-e287-5a05-a399-4b506db050ca" #> Random = "9a3f8284-a2c9-5f02-9a11-845980a1fd5c" #> Random123 = "74087812-796a-5b5d-8853-05524746bad3" #> StatsBase = "2913bbd2-ae8a-5f71-8c99-4fb6c76f3a91" #> StatsModels = "3eaba693-59b7-5ba5-a881-562e759f1c8d" #> Suppressor = "fd094767-a336-5f1f-9728-57cf17d0bbfb"

title: Calculate bootstrapped p-values of clusters — calculate_clusters_pvalues
text: empirical_clusters empirical_clusters object null_cluster_dists null_cluster_dists object add1 Whether add 1 numerator calculating probability. Use TRUE effectively count observed statistic part permuted null distribution (recommended larger nsim prior publishing results).

what: Conduct a cluster-based permutation test — clusterpermute
title: Conduct a cluster-based permutation test — clusterpermute
text: Conduct cluster-based permutation test

title: Conduct a cluster-based permutation test — clusterpermute
code: clusterpermute(   jlmer_spec,   family,   statistic,   threshold,   nsim = 100L,   predictors = NULL,   binned = FALSE,   top_n = 1L,   add1 = TRUE,   ... )

title: Conduct a cluster-based permutation test — clusterpermute
text: jlmer_spec Data prepped jlmer make_jlmer_spec() family GLM family. Currently supports "gaussian" "binomial". statistic Test statistic calculating cluster mass. Can one "t" (default) regression model output "chisq" likelihood ratio test (takes twice long calculate). threshold threshold value statistic must pass contribute cluster mass. Interpretation differs choice statistic (): statistic = "t", threshold t-value (beta/std.err) regression model. statistic = "chisq", threshold p-value chi-squared statistics likelihood ratio tests. nsim Number simulations description predictors (Optional) subset predictors test. Defaults NULL tests predictors. binned Whether data aggregated/collapsed time bins. Defaults FALSE, requires cluster span least two time points. TRUE, allows length-1 clusters exist. top_n many clusters return, order size cluster-mass statistic. Defaults Inf return detected clusters. add1 Whether add 1 numerator calculating probability. Use TRUE effectively count observed statistic part permuted null distribution (recommended larger nsim prior publishing results). ... Optional arguments passed Julia model fitting. Defaults fast = TRUE progress = FALSE.

title: Conduct a cluster-based permutation test — clusterpermute
text: empirical_clusters object p-values

title: Detect largest clusters from a time sequence of cluster statistics — extract_empirical_clusters
text: empirical_statistics predictor--time matrix empirical timewise statistics. threshold threshold value statistic must pass contribute cluster mass. Interpretation differs choice statistic (): statistic = "t", threshold t-value (beta/std.err) regression model. statistic = "chisq", threshold p-value chi-squared statistics likelihood ratio tests. binned Whether data aggregated/collapsed time bins. Defaults FALSE, requires cluster span least two time points. TRUE, allows length-1 clusters exist. top_n many clusters return, order size cluster-mass statistic. Defaults Inf return detected clusters.

title: Detect largest clusters from a time sequence of cluster statistics — extract_empirical_clusters
text: empirical_clusters object.

text: null_statistics simulation--time--predictor 3D array null (permuted) timewise statistics. threshold threshold value statistic must pass contribute cluster mass. Interpretation differs choice statistic (): statistic = "t", threshold t-value (beta/std.err) regression model. statistic = "chisq", threshold p-value chi-squared statistics likelihood ratio tests. binned Whether data aggregated/collapsed time bins. Defaults FALSE, requires cluster span least two time points. TRUE, allows length-1 clusters exist.

title: Create a specifications object for fitting regression models in Julia — make_jlmer_spec
text: formula Model formula R syntax data data frame subject Column subjects data. trial Column trials data. Must uniquely identify time series within subject (example, column items counterbalanced design subject sees exactly one item). time Column time data. drop_terms (Optional) terms drop reconstructed model formula

text: empirical_statistics predictor--time matrix empirical timewise statistics. null_statistics simulation--time--predictor 3D array null (permuted) timewise statistics. threshold_steps vector threshold values test top_n many clusters return, order size cluster-mass statistic. Defaults Inf return detected clusters. binned Whether data aggregated/collapsed time bins. Defaults FALSE, requires cluster span least two time points. TRUE, allows length-1 clusters exist. add1 Whether add 1 numerator calculating probability.
